perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video starts with a split-screen reaction to a Western female creator eating ribbon-shaped candy; the host on the right shows a surprised facial expression. (2) The scene shifts to the host in a bedroom setting, holding a small white dog and searching on a laptop. (3) The host presents two boxes of candy (red and green) to the camera with an excited expression. (4) Close-up shot of the green candy package being opened to reveal broken pieces, followed by the host's shocked facial expression with hands over her mouth. (5) The host attempts to eat the green candy, struggling to bite into its hard surface. (6) She switches to the red candy, demonstrating its elasticity and hardness, and eventually bites into a piece. (7) Final segment shows her holding the remaining candy pieces and talking directly to the camera with varying hand gestures.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) Upbeat, medium-tempo electronic background music begins. Two female voices are heard simultaneously: one in English (from the reaction video) and the host's high-pitched Korean narration. (2) A dog barks briefly in the background. (3) Sound of plastic packaging crinkling and a distinct 'snap' sound when the candy is revealed to be broken. (4) The music continues as the host's voice becomes more animated and high-pitched. (5) Clear 'crunch' and 'clack' sounds are heard as the host bites into the hard candy. (6) A cartoon-like 'whoosh' sound effect occurs during a scene transition. (7) The music remains consistent in volume as the host's talking density increases towards the end.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text: '海外網紅都在吃這個？蝴蝶結糖果' (Foreign influencers are all eating this? Ribbon candy). (2) Decorative Text: '蝴蝶結樣子的塑膠？' (Plastic shaped like a ribbon?) and '原來是糖果？！' (Turns out it's candy?!). (3) Functional Text: '發現韓國也只能網購而且要等很久...' (Found out Korea can only buy it online and wait for a long time...). (4) Decorative Text: '慌張' (Panic) and '真是無言' (Speechless) appearing as captions for emotional emphasis. (5) Functional Text: '咬不下去啊！' (Can't bite it down!) and '味道比起青葡萄更好吃！' (Tastes better than the green grape one!).

temporal_pacing_summary.mainActionFlow: Intro Reaction -> Product Search -> Unboxing -> Tasting and Verdict

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 1 main female creator, 1 female in the inset reaction clip
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: A young Korean woman with dark hair tied in a bun, wearing a bright pink knit cardigan with heart-shaped buttons.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A domestic bedroom environment featuring a bed with a blue headboard, a white pillow, a laptop, and a white Maltese-style dog.

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video is a lifestyle and food review vlog documenting the creator's pursuit and testing of a viral social media trend (ribbon candy). It transitions from reacting to a foreign influencer's video to unboxing the product and providing a humorous review based on the candy's surprising physical properties.

endorsement_analysis.endorsementCues: The creator's honest and frustrated reaction to the broken product and the difficulty of eating it suggests a genuine personal purchase rather than a paid endorsement.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Fast-paced reaction style, presence of a pet as a supporting character, and high-frequency variety-show style captions.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video effectively bridges international viral trends with local Korean availability, utilizing the 'Expectation vs. Reality' trope to engage viewers.
